stories.title: PNG cultural diversity overview
stories.content: Papua New Guinea has very high cultural and linguistic diversity. Public cultural descriptions commonly note village life, clan and family obligations, gardening, exchange ceremonies, rituals, creative expression, music, dance, carving, pottery, basketwork and cultural festivals. This record is a source note, not a community-owned tumbuna story.
stories.elderName: PNG High Commission public culture page

source.sourceTitle: Culture - High Commission of the Independent State of Papua New Guinea

stories.title: PNG cultural tours and festival overview
stories.content: Papua New Guinea tourism material describes continuing cultural practice across remote villages and urban centres, including singsing, ceremonial dress, mumu cooking, cultural festivals, markets, art and artefacts. This record is a source note for reviewer verification.
stories.elderName: Papua New Guinea Tourism Promotion Authority

source.sourceAuthor: Papua New Guinea Tourism Promotion Authority
source.license: Public webpage; verify reuse terms before republication

villages.name: Central Province language source note
villages.foundingStory: SIL's Languages of Papua New Guinea site provides province-level language references and maps. This record is a source note for language metadata and should not be treated as a village origin story.

source.sourceTitle: Languages by Province | Languages of Papua New Guinea

songs.title: Pari traditional songs archive record
songs.description: ANU Archives lists a 1979 audio recording item titled 'Pari traditional songs'. The archive page states the recording is for research purposes only and must not be reproduced without prior permission. This database record stores the existence and source of the item only, not the audio or lyrics.
songs.performer: Guba Nou, Tau Legu, Vabukori Tau, Matagu Kevau

source.license: Research purposes only; permission required for reproduction

festivals.name: Enga Cultural Show
festivals.description: Experience Enga describes the Enga Cultural Show in Wabag as an annual gathering where groups from across Enga Province showcase traditional culture through dance, rituals, crafts, skills demonstrations and singsing.

source.license: Public webpage; verify reuse terms before republication

festivals.name: Simbu and Central Highlands Festival
festivals.description: The Simbu Festival source describes participating groups from districts of the province and neighbouring provinces presenting traditional dance, dress and cultural heritage.

source.license: Public webpage; verify reuse terms before republication

festivals.name: Hiri Moale
festivals.description: The PNG High Commission culture page names Hiri Moale, held in Port Moresby every September, among PNG's popular cultural festivals.
